mechanism_narrative: Rising monthly employment levels signal expanding consumer spending capacity and corporate revenue growth, which directly supports equity earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in employment growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation compresses real yields and forces expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which increases discount rates applied to future equity cash flows and reduces forward valuations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the inflation signal, or the signal's predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing construction activity and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand, which boosts cyclical corporate earnings and drives positive equity market returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 2% over single-proxy baselines, or forward returns are flat when both proxies exceed their 12-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals capacity utilization recovery and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and drives positive earnings revisions for industrial and consumer discretionary equities.
failure_condition: Walk-forward analysis shows negative or zero correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 20-day SPX returns across three consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid labor market normalization reduces recession risk premiums and compresses equity volatility, which lifts market multiples and generates positive forward returns during late-cycle tightening phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances where the unemployment rate crosses below the 7.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with trailing corporate earnings, providing a fundamental valuation floor that supports positive equity returns regardless of short-term monetary or labor fluctuations.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward equity returns (p > 0.10) over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Prolonged zero interest rates combined with persistent inflation erode real purchasing power and signal an imminent policy pivot, which increases discount rates and weighs heavily on equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns more often than a random baseline, or single-proxy models outperform the interaction model in out-of-sample Sharpe ratios.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial output amid high demand creates margin compression and inventory buildup, which triggers negative earnings revisions and drives negative forward equity returns during supply-constrained environments.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when INDPRO declines for two consecutive months, invalidating the supply-shock transmission channel.
